title: Gel DNA Extraction
author: Kenneth Schackart
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bq8smzwe

steps.title: Perform Gel Electrophoresis on your DNA...
steps.instruction: Perform Gel Electrophoresis on your DNA sample to separate the DNA bands. The protocol recommends 1 % w/v for TBE/agarose gel and 1.2 % w/v for TAE/agarose gel. Recommended well depth is 1 mm / 1 μL sample. Other recommendations can be found in the original protocol, such as recommended voltage.

steps.title: Pre-weigh and mark 1
steps.instruction: Pre-weigh and mark 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes for each DNA band.

steps.title: Weigh the tubes with gel, and determine gel mass
steps.instruction: Weigh the tubes with gel, and determine gel mass.

steps.title: Place gel in transilluminator, and using...
steps.instruction: Place gel in transilluminator, and using a clean scalpel or blade, closely excise the desired DNA band(s). To avoid damage to the transilluminator surface, use a blunted blade. Each extraction column can process up to 1 g 1 agarose gel.

steps.title: Add 100 µL Binding Buffer per...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL Binding Buffer per 100 mg gel to centrifuge tube with gel (1:1 volume:weight). For gels with greater than 2 % w/v agarose, use 2:1 volume:weight.

steps.title: Incubate gel mixture at 55 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate gel mixture at 55 °C until gel is completely dissolved. Agitate by inversion occasionally to improve mixing. Ensure that gel is completely dissolved before proceeding.

steps.title: Check color of solution
steps.instruction: Check color of solution. A yellow color is desired. If color is orange or violet, add 10 µL of 3 Molarity (M) sodium acetate, pH 5.2 . Mix well.

steps.title: If DNA fragment length is ≤500...
steps.instruction: If DNA fragment length is ≤500 bp, add 1 gel volume of 100 % volume isopropanol to gel solution. E.g. 100 µL isopropanol to 100 mg gel in 100 µL binding buffer. Mix well. If DNA fragment length is >10 kb, add 1 gel volume water to gel solution. Mix well.

steps.title: Transfer up to 800 µL gel solution to extraction column
steps.instruction: Transfer up to 800 µL gel solution to extraction column. If gel solution volume exceeds 800 µL , add solution to column in stages; centrifuge for 1 min each time, discarding flow

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 . Always make a counter-balance when centrifuging. This is best achieved by weighing the tube and using water to make another tube of equal weight. Place same-weight pairs on opposite sides of centrifuge. A trick for getting the weight right quickly is to place tube with gel on balance, tare it, add empty tube to balance. Add 1 µL per 1 mg difference.

steps.title: Discard flow through, place column back in same collection tube
steps.instruction: Discard flow through, place column back in same collection tube.

steps.title: Add 700 µL wash buffer to purification column
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL wash buffer to purification column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 . Discard flow through. Place column back in same collection tube. Be sure to make a new counter balance for this step and all later steps since the weights will change.

steps.title: Centrifuge empty column at 14000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge empty column at 14000 x g, °C, for an additional 00:01:00 to remove any residual wash buffer. This step is not optional.

steps.title: Transfer purification column to clean 1
steps.instruction: Transfer purification column to clean 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 50 µL of Elution Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of Elution Buffer to the center of the column membrane. An elution volume between 20-50 μL can be used. Do not use less that 10 µL elution volume.

steps.title: Discard purification column
steps.instruction: Discard purification column. Store DNA at -20 °C .
